about.hero_desc: We are dedicated to business excellence, leveraging decades of experience in building and managing businesses. Our long-standing partnerships aim to empower fellow business professionals to achieve their goals, inspiring success and reinventing the way business is done.
about.vision_desc: By becoming a strategic consulting partner, we aim to bridge the gaps between businesses and their strategic goals, significantly enhancing the knowledge and capabilities of business professionals. Thus enables them to leverage their operations efficiently, ultimately generating widespread positive impacts and fostering sustainable growth for a multitude of stakeholders.
about.mission_desc: Assist businesses in navigating and overcoming challenges at every stage of their development, with a specialized focus on enhancing both their operational strategies and brand identity. Serve as a vital link between businesses and highly skilled experts who not only possess a deep theoretical understanding but also bring over 15 years of practical, hands-on experience in their respective fields.
about.speech_quote_1: We fully understand that today's businesses face a unique set of challenges... we're already overcome. Businesses now require more sophisticated new approaches to navigate the current landscape.
about.speech_quote_2: The problem is, many services in the industry today are either overly segmented, focusing only on a single issue, or too broad, often failing to address the real and pressing challenges businesses face. In most cases, the expertise offered is too limited to deliver well-rounded, effective solutions.
about.speech_quote_3: At 8vice, we bring together a diverse network of advisors whose expertise can be tailored to address specific challenges-while remaining cost-effective. Our service is designed to respond to the increasingly specialized and segmented demands of today's industries.
about.cta_desc: Tell us where you're headed in Indonesia. We'll tell you - candidly - whether and how we can help.
about.solve_card1_desc: We understand that every business is unique and each client works under a competitive environment. Our highly customized approach ensures that our solutions are tightly integrated with the specific nuances of your sector. Our approach is personalized and industry-specific, meaning we offer practical solutions to every aspect, accounting program... in various working preferences.
about.solve_card2_desc: Our ultimate aim is to identify areas and target to uncover savings by examining your operations, improving efficiency, and eliminating unnecessary expenses. Freeing your resources from fixing business problems to innovation and scale growth. This means you receive the best value for your investment, without compromising on the quality of our services.
about.solve_card3_desc: Our services are designed with your convenience in mind. With flexible scheduling for our consulting sessions and training programs, we provide multiple options to suit your specific needs. This ensures minimal disruption to your daily operations while maximizing the effectiveness of our services. Our way is out to education, creating a seamless learning experience.
about.solve_card4_desc: We maintain open communication with all of our clients. We maintain open lines of communication, providing regular updates and seeking your feedback to ensure our solutions meet your needs. This collaborative approach ensures that we are always on the same page and working towards your goals.

services_page.step1_desc: A candid 45-minute call. We map your situation and tell you honestly whether we can help.
services_page.step2_desc: We define a project sized to your decision and assemble the senior operators best suited to it.
services_page.step3_desc: We do the work alongside your team - diagnostics, licensing, partnerships, hiring, whatever it takes.
services_page.step4_desc: You leave with progress you can take to your board, and a local team capable of carrying it.
services_page.ind_desc: Our bench spans consumer, financial, technology, industrial and resource sectors - so the operator advising you has actually worked in your industry, in this market.

services_page.cta_desc: Tell us the decision in front of you. We'll point you to the right service - or tell you that you don't need us yet.

case_study_page.hero_title: A Chinese fintech, live in six months.
case_study_page.hero_desc: A payments company with strong product and real capital had stalled twice trying to enter Indonesia. We compressed a typically 18-month entry into six - licensing, structure, and a working local team.
case_study_page.challenge_head: Twice stalled, and burning a quarter each time.
case_study_page.challenge_p1: The client had everything except a path. Two earlier attempts - one with a global consultancy, one with a local fixer - had produced a structure they couldn't operate under and a distributor relationship that went nowhere.
case_study_page.challenge_p2: By the time they reached us, the headquarters in Shenzhen had lost confidence that Indonesia was workable at all. The product was proven elsewhere in Southeast Asia. The capital was committed. What was missing was a sequence that respected how Indonesian licensing actually gates a payments business - and a team on the ground who had done it before.
case_study_page.step1_desc: Four weeks pulling apart the two failed attempts to find the real blocker - it was ordering, not strategy.
case_study_page.step2_desc: Pinned the exact activity and KBLI, confirmed ownership headroom, and sequenced permits so none waited on another.
case_study_page.step3_desc: Designed the PT PMA to the licensing reality - not the other way round - and unwound the unusable prior entity.
case_study_page.step4_desc: Hired and onboarded 14 people for the market, with a country lead who could carry it without us.
case_study_page.quote_main: "They told us in week two that our previous structure had to go. It stung - and it was the most valuable thing anyone had said to us in a year."
case_study_page.outcome_p1: Six months after the first call, the company was operating under a correctly-scoped license, with a local team running day-to-day and a country lead reporting cleanly into Shenzhen. The structure that had taken two prior attempts to get wrong took one focused engagement to get right.
case_study_page.outcome_p2: More importantly, the headquarters got its confidence back. Indonesia went from "the market we couldn't crack" to a live operation with a credible growth plan - and a board that could finally see the path.
case_study_page.dir_feat_brief: Restructured an inefficient legacy supply chain in Indonesia, unlocking massive operational savings and clearing the runway for local IPO.
case_study_page.cta_desc: Most stalls are sequencing problems, not strategy problems. Let us take an honest look.

article_page.hero_desc: How cross-border trade is adapting to the new regulatory landscape.
article_page.newsletter_desc: Get raw, unvarnished insights from our partners on the ground.
article_page.p1: When Chinese electric vehicle manufacturers first looked at Indonesia, the strategy was purely extractive: secure nickel, process it, and ship it back...
article_page.p2: Indonesia classifies every business activity under a KBLI code, and your permitted foreign ownership, your minimum capital, and your licensing burden all flow from that code. Choose the company structure before you've pinned the exact KBLI - and confirmed it against the latest investment list - and you can find yourself legally incorporated but unable to actually do the thing you came to do.
article_page.p3: We've watched companies spend four months and real money unwinding a structure that was technically valid and strategically useless. The fix is not more diligence at the end. It's a different sequence at the start.
article_page.quote: The report telling them what to do was never wrong. It was just never written by anyone who'd have to live with the consequences.
article_page.p4: When we run a market-entry diagnostic, the licensing order looks like this - and it rarely matches the checklist a client arrives with:
article_page.li1: Pin the exact activity, then the KBLI. Not the approximate one. The specific sub-code that governs ownership and capital for what you'll really be doing on day one and in year three.
article_page.li2: Confirm foreign-ownership headroom. Check the activity against the current Positive Investment List before any structure is drawn. This is where assumptions quietly break.
article_page.li3: Design the structure to the code. Only now decide on PT PMA shape, capital, and any local partnership - because now you know the constraints you're designing within.
article_page.li4: Sequence the permits. Some licenses gate others. Map the dependency chain so you're never waiting on a permit you could have filed two months earlier.
article_page.p5: The assumption almost everyone makes is that licensing is an administrative task to be completed - a box to tick after the strategy is set. It isn't. In Indonesia, the licensing reality is part of the strategy. What you're permitted to own, where, and how fast, should shape the business plan, not merely follow it.
article_page.p6: Companies that internalise this stop treating market entry as a translation problem and start treating it as a design problem. That shift - more than any single permit - is what compresses an 18-month entry into six.
article_page.p7: If you're early, do one thing before anything else: write down, in a single sentence, exactly what your Indonesian entity will do on its first day of operation. Then bring that sentence to someone who can map it to a KBLI code and an ownership reality. Almost everything else follows from getting that one sentence right.
article_page.p8: That's the work we do in the first phase of every consultancy engagement - and often, it's the work that changes the plan.
article_page.aside_cta_desc: Bring us your one-sentence activity. We'll map the KBLI and ownership reality in your free consultancy call.

consultancy_page.hero_desc: We assemble a small, senior team around your specific situation - then stay through execution. Market entry, expansion, or turnaround, led by operators who've done it here before.
consultancy_page.approach_p1: Most firms either generalise - advice you've heard before - or hyper-specialise, handing you one piece of the puzzle instead of the puzzle. We do neither.
consultancy_page.approach_p2: For each engagement we draw from a network of operators who've spent decades inside the specific industry you're entering, and build a team scoped to the decision in front of you. You get depth where it matters and nothing you don't need.
consultancy_page.cap1_desc: A clear-eyed read on whether, when, and how to enter - sized, sequenced, and costed.
consultancy_page.cap2_desc: OSS, KBLI classification, the negative investment list, and the permits foreign owners trip on.
consultancy_page.cap3_desc: Hiring for the market, not the résumé - leadership, structure, and incentives that hold.
consultancy_page.cap4_desc: Channel design and partner selection built around real Indonesian buying behaviour.
consultancy_page.cap5_desc: Localising value and price for a 270-million-person market that is anything but uniform.
consultancy_page.cap6_desc: Knowing who you're actually getting into business with, before the ink dries.
consultancy_page.cap7_desc: Making Mandarin-first HQ and Indonesian operations genuinely work together.
consultancy_page.cap8_desc: Resilient sourcing, production and logistics across Java, Sumatra and beyond.
consultancy_page.cap9_desc: When an entry has stalled, we diagnose honestly and rebuild the path forward.
consultancy_page.process_title: How we work, step by step.
consultancy_page.step1_desc: A free, candid call. We pressure-test your assumptions and define the real question.
consultancy_page.step2_desc: A written proposal with a fixed scope, team, timeline and price - sized to your decision.
consultancy_page.step3_desc: We embed with your team and do the work, with weekly checkpoints and zero surprises.
consultancy_page.step4_desc: Board-ready outcomes and a local team able to carry the work without us.
consultancy_page.faq_desc: The questions every prospective client asks before we begin. If yours isn't here, the answer is one call away.
consultancy_page.faq1_q: How long is a typical engagement?
consultancy_page.faq1_a: It depends on the decision. A focused market-entry diagnostic runs four to six weeks. A full entry - from licensing through first local hires - is usually a three-to-six-month engagement. Turnarounds vary with the depth of the problem. We scope to the work, never to a retainer clock.
consultancy_page.faq2_q: Do you stay through execution, or just advise?
consultancy_page.faq2_a: We stay. The whole point of 8vice is that the operators who diagnose the problem are the ones who help you fix it. We embed alongside your team rather than handing over a report and walking away.
consultancy_page.faq3_a: Project-based and fixed at scoping. You'll know the price before any work begins, and it won't drift. For longer programs we agree milestone-based phases so you can stop or extend at natural decision points.
consultancy_page.faq4_q: Can you work with our Mandarin-first HQ?
consultancy_page.faq4_a: Yes. Our China Desk works in Mandarin with your headquarters while our Indonesian operators work locally - so nothing is lost in translation between the two sides of your business.
consultancy_page.faq5_q: What if you decide we shouldn't proceed?
consultancy_page.faq5_a: Then we'll tell you - clearly and early. Talking a client out of a bad move is part of the service. Our reputation in this corridor is worth more than any single engagement fee.
consultancy_page.case_title: A Chinese fintech, live in six months.
consultancy_page.case_desc: From licensing pathway to a working local operating team - see how a focused engagement compressed a typically 18-month entry into half the time.
consultancy_page.cta_desc: Bring us the decision. We'll give you an honest read within two business days.

mi_page.hero_desc: We deliver precise, actionable market intelligence that replaces guesswork with ground truth. Understand the Indonesian landscape before you commit capital.
mi_page.approach_p1: We combine rigorous quantitative analysis with deep on-the-ground qualitative research to deliver actionable market intelligence. Our analysts work across Indonesia's major urban centers and emerging regional markets to provide a complete picture.
mi_page.approach_p2: Every engagement begins with understanding your strategic objectives, then we tailor our research framework to deliver insights that directly inform your go-to-market decisions in Indonesia.
mi_page.cap1_desc: Data-backed TAM, SAM, and SOM analysis with 3–5 year projections tailored to Indonesia's unique regional dynamics across Java, Sumatra, and Kalimantan.
mi_page.cap2_desc: Deep-dive into Indonesia's evolving regulatory landscape including BKPM requirements, negative investment lists, sector-specific licenses, and regional autonomy implications.
mi_page.cap3_desc: Comprehensive mapping of local and international competitors, pricing strategies, distribution networks, and market share analysis across key Indonesian sectors.
mi_page.cap4_desc: Qualitative and quantitative research decoding Indonesian consumer behavior, cultural nuances, purchasing power, and brand perception.
mi_page.cap5_desc: Assessment of local manufacturing capabilities, logistics infrastructure bottlenecks, and viable sourcing networks for your operations.
mi_page.cap6_desc: Identification and vetting of potential joint-venture partners, distributors, or acquisition targets that align with your strategic goals.
mi_page.step1_desc: We define the specific strategic decisions our research needs to inform, agreeing on key metrics and scope.
mi_page.step2_desc: Our local analysts gather primary data through interviews, surveys, and site visits, supplemented by robust secondary research.
mi_page.step3_desc: We distill complex data into clear, actionable insights, highlighting risks and identifying concrete opportunities.
mi_page.step4_desc: A comprehensive presentation of findings and strategic recommendations, delivered to your leadership team.
mi_page.scope1_desc: A high-level synthesis of key findings presented directly to your decision-makers.
mi_page.scope2_desc: Detailed analysis covering all agreed intelligence pillars with supporting data.
mi_page.scope3_desc: Clear, actionable next steps based on our findings and local expertise.
mi_page.scope4_desc: Full access to the raw data, survey results, and interview transcripts collected during fieldwork.
mi_page.case_desc: Identified key partnership opportunities with local logistics providers and digital payment gateways for a leading Chinese retail platform entering the Indonesian market.
mi_page.cta_desc: Let's talk data. We'll scope your intelligence needs within two business days.

training_page.cap5_desc: Build resilient sourcing, logistics, and distribution networks tailored to Indonesia's unique geographical challenges.

training_page.cap7_desc: Align your operations with local ESG regulations and consumer expectations for sustainable business practices.

training_page.cap9_desc: A complete, sequenced roadmap taking you from strategic planning to successful market entry and product launch.
